description:
Goal: the work graph should wake dependents when fact values change, not when unrelated producer revision counters happen to increase. Signal: existing single-owner presence facts behave exactly as before, and tests prove multi-owner value facts aggregate by content with stable fingerprints. Strategy: replace u64-only fact contributions with FactValue, keep Presence(u64) as today's semantics, and add the lattice machinery needed for activation inputs before any activation fact starts using it.

Change:
- Introduce FactValue with Presence(u64) for existing facts and Inputs(Vec<Ty>) for activation input facts.
- Give FactValue a family-specific join and content fingerprint.
- Change JobEffects outputs and FactTable replacement to aggregate values and report changes on fingerprint change.
- Keep current facts single-owner/presence during this ticket so behavior stays green.

Why:
- max(revision) is only honest while every fact has one owner. Activation input will be multi-owner, so the fact table must become value-aware first.

Checks:
- Scheduler/fact tests cover unchanged presence behavior, contribution retraction, and content-stable multi-owner joins.
- Compiler2 quicksort and Enum.reduce tests remain green before activation input moves.
title: Compiler2 facts: make fact slots value-based
